name: TCMalloc
summary: Google's per-CPU thread-caching allocator with a hugepage-aware Temeraire backend that maximises 2 MiB-page coverage for TLB efficiency across a fleet of millions of machines.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Google's TCMalloc — the modern open-source reincarnation, separate from gperftools — is optimised for *aggregate* CPU cost across millions of machines, not per-allocation throughput on a single core. It uses per-CPU (not just per-thread) caches and a hugepage-aware page heap (Temeraire) that maximises 2 MiB-page coverage for TLB efficiency across the fleet.

Alongside jemalloc, TCMalloc is the dominant fleet-scale allocator. The OSDI 2021 Temeraire paper documents the production CPU savings.

sections.title: Per-CPU caches and hugepage-aware backend
sections.content:
Per-CPU mode uses Linux restartable sequences (`rseq`) to access cache state without atomics or per-thread storage explosion. The cache count is bounded by CPU count rather than thread count, which matters at the scale where a single binary may host thousands of goroutine-style threads.

Temeraire's `HugeFiller`, `HugeCache`, `HugeAllocator`, and `HugeRegion` heuristics pack allocations onto already-full hugepages while keeping others fully empty so they can be returned to the OS. Hot/cold size-class hints split the page heap: cold allocations get `MADV_NOHUGEPAGE` and live in a separate region, enabling memory tiering. The backend cooperates explicitly with the OS page-size hierarchy (4 KiB to 2 MiB to 1 GiB), which is what makes Temeraire pay off at fleet scale.

sections.title: Deployment
sections.content: Status (as of 2026-04): documented fleet-wide deployment per Google's published descriptions across search, YouTube, and Borg jobs; exact rollout share is vendor- and time-specific. The OSDI 2021 paper documents fleet-CPU savings from Temeraire enablement. TCMalloc is the production existence proof that allocator designs cooperating with the OS page-size hierarchy outperform single-page-size assumptions at scale.
